blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: I'll need a formal statement from you. Tomorrow morning, at the station.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: And I'll need you not to discuss this with anyone — specifically not with anyone from that house.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I haven't spoken to them. I don't know them.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Keep it that way for now.
He put his hat back on. Started down the path.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: The window. In the room where he was found.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: It was a cold night. Forty-seven degrees. A man goes upstairs to rest for an hour. He locks the door — from the inside. He takes off his shoes. He lies down on top of the covers.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: A man who lies down for an hour in a cold house does not open his window all the way. Not unless he's very warm. Not unless something in his body is making him very warm.
Davenport stood on the path between the two houses and said nothing.
He went inside. Left Davenport standing there in the dark with his hat in his hands.
